text: OIL SURGES 8%: Brent $109.16 (+8.0%), WTI $108.36 (+8.2%) — Trump escalation vow reverses April 1 peace dip; Asia markets plunge (Nikkei -1.4%, Kospi -2.82%); gas now $4.064/gal (AAA)

sources.name: CNBC — Oil surges on Trump speech

text: IRGC THREATENS 18 US TECH GIANTS: Apple, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla, Boeing listed as targets — orders employee evacuations; unprecedented corporate infrastructure targeting

UK 35-NATION HORMUZ SUMMIT TODAY: FM Cooper chairing virtual meeting (France, Germany, Italy, Canada, UAE, Japan) — US NOT attending; goal: restore Strait freedom of navigation; military planners to follow

DAY 34 AM: Brent $109/WTI $108 (surging +8%); Iran 4 salvos overnight (cluster warheads, 4 hurt); Hezbollah 50+ rockets; IRGC threatens tech companies; UK 35-nation Hormuz summit; China-Pakistan peace plan; gas $4.064; Rubio-Hegseth ground troop feud; Argentina designates IRGC terrorist

PASTEUR INSTITUTE STRUCK: US-Israeli airstrike destroys century-old medical research center in Tehran — Iran's Health Ministry: 'barbaric assault on international health security'; 316 healthcare centers damaged since Feb 28
US EMBASSY BAGHDAD: IMMINENT MILITIA ATTACKS WITHIN 24-48 HOURS — US citizens told to leave Iraq immediately; airspace closed; targets include diplomatic facilities, energy infrastructure, hotels

UK HORMUZ SUMMIT: 40 nations attend (expanded from 35) — signed statement demanding Iran reopen strait; US notably absent; follow-up military planning pledged; Iran-Oman separately finalize draft navigation regime
GUTERRES: 'EDGE OF WIDER WAR' — UN chief directly addresses US/Israel: 'high time to stop the war'; dispatches envoy Jean Arnault to region; Macron calls Hormuz military op 'unrealistic'

sources.name: UN News — Guterres wider war warning

text: DAY 34 PM: Brent $109/WTI $111 (volatile +8-13%); gas $4.08 (AAA); Pasteur Institute struck; Baghdad militia warning; 40-nation Hormuz summit; Iran-Oman navigation draft; Guterres 'edge of wider war'; 2 IDF killed in Lebanon; stocks S&P -0.81%; gold plunges -2.7%

HEGSETH FIRES ARMY CHIEF: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth fired Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George effective immediately — replaced by military aide Gen. LaNeve; major leadership upheaval during active wartime operations

Washington Post — Hegseth ousts Gen. George

text: EVENING SALVO: Iran fires ~30 missiles at Israel — Petah Tikva industrial zone struck; cluster bomblets spread to playground; 11-year-old girl critically hurt; Houthi missile at Tel Aviv intercepted; 50+ Hezbollah rockets at northern Israel

Times of Israel — Girl critically hurt by cluster bomb

text: DAY 34 EVE: Brent $109.35/WTI $111 (volatile +8-13%); gas $4.08; markets recovered (Dow -0.13%, S&P +0.11%); Army chief fired; Petah Tikva cluster strike; 40-nation Hormuz summit; Macron 'unrealistic' to reopen militarily; Hegseth fires Gen. George; Houthi + Hezbollah evening attacks

RUBIO: 'FINISH LINE' — Secretary of State says US can see end of Iran war: 'It's not today, it's not tomorrow, but it is coming'; confirms message exchanges with Iran; hints at direct meeting potential
GCC BACKS UN FORCE RESOLUTION: Gulf nations back UNSC resolution authorizing 'all defensive means necessary' to reopen Hormuz — Bahrain proposed; Russia/China expected to veto; vote FRIDAY April 4

DATED BRENT HITS $141.36: Physical crude spot price highest since 2008 financial crisis; WTI settles $113.03 after 12.9% single-session surge — biggest daily gain since war began; IEA warns April 'much worse than March'
IDF 162ND DIVISION: 40 Hezbollah fighters killed in southern Lebanon advance — IDF reaches new section of Litani River; 7 civilians killed; Lebanese Army withdraws from border villages
TRUMP: 'BRIDGES NEXT, THEN POWER PLANTS' — Truth Social post threatens next phase of Iran infrastructure destruction; April 6 Hormuz deadline unchanged; 'hasn't even started destroying what's left'

sources.name: Al-Monitor — Trump threatens bridges and power plants

text: DAY 35 AM: Brent $109.35/WTI $113.03 (physical Brent $141!); gas $4.08; Trump threatens bridges + power plants; GCC backs UN Hormuz force vote Friday; Rubio 'finish line'; IDF kills 40 Hezbollah in Lebanon; IRGC hits Amazon Bahrain 4th time; April 6 deadline 3 DAYS AWAY
HEZBOLLAH 130 ROCKETS: Massive Passover barrage at northern Israel — 30+ rockets in single salvo targeting Kiryat Ata/Haifa area; 4 lightly injured; largest single barrage this week
IRAN CLUSTER BOMBS: Ballistic missiles with cluster munitions hit Kiryat Ata — 1 wounded; IDF warns of escalating Iranian weapon types; cluster warheads banned under Convention on Cluster Munitions
KUWAIT DOUBLE HIT: Drones strike Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery (466K bpd — 3rd time); missile hits desalination plant — Kuwait blames Iran; IRGC denies; fires contained at refinery

text: IRAN CLAIMS F-15E SHOOTDOWN: IRGC says US jet downed near Qeshm Island in Hormuz — US CENTCOM denies: 'all aircraft safe'; debris photos show F-15E wreckage not F-35 as Iran initially claimed

sources.name: The Aviationist — Iran F-15E debris analysis

text: HEGSETH FIRES ARMY CHIEF: Gen. Randy George + 2 generals ousted during wartime; Gen. LaNeve (Hegseth's former aide) becomes acting chief; Hegseth: '15 ways we could come at them with boots on the ground'

text: TRUMP: 'DO WHAT I TELL THEM' — says Israel will stop attacking Iran 'when I stop'; asserts full US control over war; reiterates 'bridges next, then power plants'; April 6 Hormuz deadline unchanged

DAY 35 MIDDAY: Brent ~$109/WTI ~$112; gas $4.09; 130 Hezbollah rockets; Iran cluster bombs; Kuwait refinery+desal plant hit; Iran claims F-15E shootdown (denied); Hegseth fires Army chief; Trump asserts full war control; April 6 deadline 3 DAYS
